title: 'podman exec --tty --user bar ...' fails with crun error about 'chown /dev/pts/0'
body:
### Issue Description

This was discovered by the Fedora CoreOS folks at https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1908 with a nightly build of Fedora Rawhide, but it also affects Fedora Workstation.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Create a container `foo`:
```ShellSession
$ podman create --name foo registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:43 sleep +Inf
94d93d673ca79d02321bbcfe4c674e23233f0162a70a1fb73bb25abc36810827
```

Start it and create a user `bar` inside it:
```ShellSession
$ podman start foo
foo
$ podman exec foo useradd bar
```

Try to `exec` into the container:
```ShellSession
$ podman exec --tty --user bar foo true
Error: crun: chown `/dev/pts/0`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
```

It can also be easily reproduced with any Toolbx container:
```ShellSession
$ toolbox create
Created container: fedora-toolbox-43
Enter with: toolbox enter
$ toolbox --verbose enter
...
...
Error: crun: chown `/dev/pts/0`: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
Error: failed to invoke command /bin/bash in container fedora-toolbox-43
```

### Describe the results you received

`podman exec --tty --user bar ...` fails with a `crun(1)` error about `chown /dev/pts/0`.

### Describe the results you expected

`podman exec --tty --user bar ...` shouldn't error out.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Fedora Rawhide.

One can try the Workstation variant with [this](https://kojipkgs.fedoraproject.org/compose/rawhide/Fedora-Rawhide-20250401.n.0/compose/Workstation/x86_64/iso/Fedora-Workstation-Live-Rawhide-20250401.n.0.x86_64.iso) nightly ISO.

### Additional information

n.a.

comments.body: As @debarshiray mentioned we [found this](https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/issues/1908) in CoreOS land by testing bodhi updates in rawhide. There was some change in kernel 6.15 that caused this regression. Maybe `podman` needs to adjust somewhere for the change in behavior? Or maybe the fix needs to be in the kernel specifically?
Xref to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25738.  Likely a kernel issue.

That looks like a kernel bug in 6.15 and not something specific to podman,
I think I run into the same thing in the podman tests directly (slightly different command but same error), https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25738#issuecomment-2769241458
yes that is the same issue

it is confirmed to be a kernel issue, fixed with https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/759134.1743596274@warthog.procyon.org.uk/T/#u


I think we can close the issue because there is nothing to do from userspace
Thanks for tracking it down and notifying the kernel folks!
(in case anyone wonders the patch is not part of 6.15-rc2, but it looks like it was merged in Linus tree so I assume it will be part of rc3)
